questions.question: A city salesman has 6 cities to visit and the distances between the cities are as follows: City A to B (10), City A to C (15), City B to C (35), City A to D (20), City B to D (30), City C to D (25). What is the minimum distance the salesman can travel to visit all cities and return to the starting city?
questions.hint: Use the nearest neighbour algorithm and compare the result with the brute force method

questions.question: A graph has 7 vertices and the following edges: (1,2), (1,3), (2,4), (3,5), (4,6), (5,7). Find a Hamiltonian path in the graph.
questions.hint: Start from vertex 1 and try to find a path that visits all vertices without repeating any vertex

questions.question: A travelling salesman has to visit 5 cities: City A, City B, City C, City D, and City E, and the distances between the cities are as follows: City A to B (20), City A to C (15), City B to C (30), City B to D (40), City C to D (25), City C to E (10), City D to E (35). What is the minimum distance the salesman can travel to visit all cities and return to the starting city?
questions.hint: Use the nearest neighbour algorithm to find a solution
